# OPC UA Laravel Client

> Laravel integration for OPC UA built on opcua-php-client and opcua-php-client-session-manager. Provides a Facade, .env-based configuration, named connections, and an Artisan command for the session manager daemon.

## Use Cases

- Read and write process variables from PLCs, SCADA systems, sensors via a Laravel Facade
- Browse OPC UA address spaces and resolve paths from Laravel controllers, jobs, or commands
- Subscribe to data changes and events with session persistence across HTTP requests
- Call methods on OPC UA servers from a Laravel application
- Query historical data (raw, processed, at-time) from historians

## Key Features

- Facade (`Opcua::read()`, `Opcua::browse()`, etc.) with full IDE autocompletion
- Named connections — define multiple OPC UA servers in config/opcua.php
- Ad-hoc connections — Opcua::connectTo() for runtime endpoints
- Transparent session management — daemon available → ManagedClient; not → direct Client
- String NodeIds everywhere — 'i=2259', 'ns=2;s=MyNode'
- Fluent Builder API — readMulti(), writeMulti(), createMonitoredItems(), translateBrowsePaths()
- PSR-3 Logging — Laravel log channel injected automatically
- PSR-16 Caching — Laravel cache store injected automatically, per-call useCache
- Type Discovery — discoverDataTypes() for server-defined structured types
- Subscription Transfer — transferSubscriptions() / republish() for session recovery
- MockClient for testing — register handlers, assert calls, no server needed
- Timeout, auto-retry, automatic batching via config or fluent API
- Recursive browsing with depth control and cycle detection
- Path resolution — resolveNodeId('/Objects/Server/ServerStatus')
- 6 security policies, 3 auth modes, auto-generated certificates
- Historical data — raw, processed, and at-time queries
- All service responses return typed DTOs (SubscriptionResult, CallResult, BrowseResultSet, etc.)

## Installation

```bash
composer require gianfriaur/opcua-laravel-client
```

## Quick Example

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaLaravel\Facades\Opcua;

$client = Opcua::connect();
$value = $client->read('i=2259');
echo $value->getValue(); // 0 = Running
$client->disconnect();
```

## Fluent Builder API

```php
$results = $client->readMulti()
    ->node('i=2259')->value()
    ->node('ns=2;i=1001')->displayName()
    ->execute();
```

## Session Manager

```bash
php artisan opcua:session --log-channel=stack --cache-store=redis
```

## Main Classes

- Gianfriaur\OpcuaLaravel\OpcuaManager — connection management, client creation, configuration
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaLaravel\Facades\Opcua — static Facade with full PHPDoc
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaLaravel\OpcuaServiceProvider — service container registration
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaLaravel\Commands\SessionCommand — Artisan command for daemon

## Related Packages

- gianfriaur/opcua-php-client — Pure PHP OPC UA client (core protocol)
- gianfriaur/opcua-php-client-session-manager — Daemon-based session persistence
- opcua-test-server-suite — Docker-based OPC UA test servers

## Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- ext-openssl
- Laravel 11.x or 12.x
